page: Microsoft Azure M96bds_2_v3 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the MBDSMediumMemoryv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8473C processor operating at 4.0 GHz, with 48 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). This configuration is equipped with 1946.0 GB of system memory, yielding 20.27 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage consists of 4608 GB of bundled capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs are included in this server type.

In benchmark evaluations, the M96bds_2_v3 demonstrates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, achieving top 10% placement in both cached small-block read-only (7459.57 GB/sec) and uncached large-block read/write tests. Multi-threaded processing is highly efficient, with Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 10%. The hardware-assisted matrix and vector extensions contribute to strong top 25% performance in LLM prompt processing across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds for 135M and 70B models fall into the middle 50% average. Database operations are highly performant, with Passmark database operations scoring in the top 10% and Redis SET operations reaching 114.97 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of memory relative to compute resources makes this instance suitable for workloads requiring massive in-memory datasets without the overhead of scaling out to multiple smaller nodes. The combination of 1946.0 GB of RAM, 4608 GB of local storage, and high memory bandwidth aligns with the demands of large-scale relational databases, in-memory data stores, and heavy enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems. It is also well-suited for multi-threaded data processing tasks, such as software compilation and complex physical simulations, where the 96 dedicated vCPUs can be fully utilized.
description: Microsoft Azure M96bds_2_v3 is a memory-optimized server from the MBDSMediumMemoryv3 family, featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8473C processor running at 4.0 GHz. It is equipped with 1946.0 GB of RAM, providing 20.27 GB per core, and includes 4608 GB of local storage. The architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. In benchmark testing, the server exhibits top-tier memory bandwidth, multi-core CPU performance, and database operations. While single-core CPU performance and certain LLM text generation tasks fall into average ranges, its massive memory capacity and high multi-threaded throughput make it highly effective for large-memory databases, in-memory analytics, and intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M96bds_2_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1946 GB of RAM, and 4608 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86 platform featuring massive RAM capacity, high-speed local storage, and top-tier multi-threaded processing for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 4.0 GHz with AMX and AVX-512 support.
1946.0 GB of system memory delivering top-tier read and write bandwidth.
4608 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks and database operations.
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise ERP systems.
